source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
We have a large number of blocks of $1 \mathrm{~cm} \times$ $2 \mathrm{~cm} \times 4 \mathrm{~cm}$. We will try to put as many of these blocks as possible into a box of $4 \mathrm{~cm} \times$ $4 \mathrm{~cm} \times 4 \mathrm{~cm}$ so that we were able to close the box with a lid. How many blocks fit in?
<image1>

image_dependency: question text fully states all needed dimensions (1x2x4 blocks and 4x4x4 box)
an image file exists in the registry, but no visual attributes or spatial reading from the image are referenced in the question
multi_step: the task asks for the maximum number of blocks that fit, which implies optimization rather than direct lookup
solving from text likely requires combining box dimensions and block dimensions
a typical solution involves at least checking volume capacity and whether the dimensions permit a packing arrangement
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value: 8
the dimensions in the question are explicit, making independent calculation/checking feasible

decision_reasons: question, answer, and image assets are all present with no registry issues
the problem is highly verifiable because the dimensions are explicit and the answer is a single numeric value
the text appears sufficient to solve the problem even though an image is included, so core semantics are fully recoverable
multi-step reasoning demand is moderate, which supports candidate value for the pool
the only risk flag is that the image may be unnecessary, which does not undermine asset completeness or solvability
